extraction.one_line_summary: DENZ Nico won from a 30-rider breakaway after surviving the final climb by teeth-gritted determination, then outsprinting Squinzi and Berwick in Rivoli
tactical_patterns: Five riders split from 30-man break with 90km remaining, creating decisive selection before final climb commenced.
BATISTELLO Samuel dropped despite making initial five-man selection, illness trumping positioning advantage in extended breakaways.
DENZ attacked 12km out on Colle Braida while visibly suffering, revealing aggression-while-weak can hold off fresher chasers.

rider_signals.GROVES Kaden: negative: abandoned race ill from back of peloton, never rejoined after start
rider_signals.MILAN Jonathan: neutral: required doctor treatment mid-race but continued, points jersey ambitions compromised
rider_signals.DENZ Nico: positive: first grand tour win at 29 after nearly being dropped on final climb, BORA's depth paying dividends
rider_signals.BATISTELLO Samuel: negative: made five-man selection but illness forced him back to peloton, positioning couldn't overcome physical state
